page: The OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 256 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided by a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 16 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark evaluations indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 10%. The system demonstrates strong memory bandwidth, particularly in cached read operations, which also ranks in the top 10%. Software compilation via Clang, ray tracing, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also achieve top-tier ratings. Single-core performance, text processing, and database operations on PassMark fall into the strong top 25% category.

Given its dedicated 64-core architecture and high memory bandwidth, the b3-256 is highly efficient for parallel processing tasks, software compilation, and database operations. The lack of hyperthreading ensures predictable performance per core, making it suitable for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and moderate LLM prompt processing. The bundled NVMe storage and high-density resource profile offer a cost-effective solution for enterprise deployments requiring dedicated CPU resources without the overhead of virtualization-induced thread sharing.
description: The OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose server configured with an AMD EPYC Milan processor featuring 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 threads. Operating at 2.3 GHz, this x86_64 system is equipped with 256 GB of RAM, offering a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is handled by a 400 GB NVMe SSD, complemented by a 16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks highlight top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and cached memory bandwidth, alongside strong database operations and static web serving throughput. Lacking hyperthreading, the dedicated core allocation ensures highly predictable performance. This configuration is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software development pipelines, database hosting, and parallel processing workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose server featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and 16 Gbps network bandwidth.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose server with 64 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated sixty-four core platform delivering top-tier multi-threaded performance and high-speed NVMe storage for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores with no hyperthreading
256 GB of system memory paired with 400 GB local NVMe SSD storage
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and one public IPv4 address
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and software compilation speeds
Strong database operations and cached memory read bandwidth
